title: lavoisiera gouveana
intro: Lavoisiera gouveana is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the logan apple, which is a small shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lavoisiera gouveana include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Lavoisiera gouveana is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used in topical applications to take advantage of its antiseptic properties.
